ux_issues.description: Companion mode message triggered create_itinerary_items tool call for the evening suggestion (hot springs + dinner). While helpful, this could clutter the itinerary with ad-hoc companion suggestions mixed in with the planned itinerary. Users might not expect companion chat to modify their itinerary.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider making itinerary additions in companion mode opt-in or clearly distinguishing ad-hoc companion suggestions from the planned itinerary.

ux_issues.description: The shared trip endpoint returns a JSON array at the top level rather than an object with a 'trip' key, which is inconsistent with GetTrip which returns {"trip": {...}}. The shared view does include trip and itinerary data though.
ux_issues.suggestion: Wrap shared trip response in an object for consistency with the rest of the API.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No expert persona handoff was triggered despite asking specifically about wildlife watching and frog tours for kids, which should have matched a nature/family expert. The AI answered well without handoff, but the persona system was not exercised.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Asked about best places to see frogs and wildlife with kids, guided night tours safe for a 6-year-old. AI gave a detailed and helpful response but did not call suggest_expert.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: In the itinerary creation response, the AI said 'all those fantastic activities for days 8-10 are already saved in your itinerary' as if referring to previously created items, but this was the first itinerary creation call. The phrasing was slightly confusing — the AI created items in two batches (days 1-7 then 8-10) via tool loop, but the final text message only acknowledged the second batch.
ai_behavior_issues.context: The tool created 18 items in the first call and 5 in the second call. The AI's final visible response only mentioned the second batch (days 8-10), making it seem like the first batch was pre-existing.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The trip creation and itinerary building were excellent. The AI created a well-structured 10-day Costa Rica itinerary with realistic driving times, age-appropriate activities, and a sensible pace (3 locations, 3-4 nights each). Safety information was specific and practical — medical facilities were named by location, water safety advice was tailored to Noah's inability to swim. Driving logistics response included realistic time estimates and confirmed the 4x4 recommendation. Companion mode gave concise, actionable advice including a specific restaurant recommendation for dinner. The only notable gap was no expert persona handoff despite a clear nature/wildlife topic opportunity. Context retention was perfect — the AI never asked where we were going after trip creation.
